sections.paragraph:
fut. יִבְכֶּה convers. וַיֵּבְךְּ pr. i.q. בָּכָא to distil, to flow by drops (the primary syllable בך imitates the sound of falling drops, comp. the roots ending with ךְ); see בְּכִי , specially, to weep, a root common to all the cognate languages and dialects, Exo 2:6 Gen 43:30 2Sa 19:2 often used of the people lamenting in public calamities, Num 11:10 25:6 used of the mourning of penitents, Ezr 10:1. Followed by an acc. to weep for, bewail any one, especially the dead. Gen 23:2 37:35 50:3 also followed by עַל of the person or thing wept for, Lam 1:16 Jud. 11:37 אֶל 2Sa 1:24 Eze 27:31 and לְ Jer 22:10 Job 30:25. בֳּכָה followed by עַל is also to come to any one weeping, Num 11:13 Jud. 14:16 and, to weep upon any one, i.e. in his embrace, Gen 45:15 50:1 .

Piel, to bewail, weep for the dead, with an acc. Jer 31:15 Eze 8:14.

Derived nouns are בֶּכֶה, בָּכוּת, בְּכִי, בְּכִית.
